title: Sharing a Tool That Keeps ChatGPT WebUI Running for NeuroGolf Tasks
summary: Public discussion describes a ChatGPT WebUI Bridge for long-running NeuroGolf agent work.
Architecture described in the post: local backend plus Tampermonkey userscript connected to logged-in ChatGPT WebUI sessions.
Use case: keep long-running search/experimentation loops moving without manually monitoring many ChatGPT windows.
Direct score impact: no ONNX model, submission zip, task-specific rule, or dataset link was provided in the discussion content fetched here.
E-team use: record as workflow/automation idea only; do not treat it as a model source for scoring.

comment_summaries.summary: Comment asks whether this WebUI automation could be restricted by GPT anti-abuse mechanisms.
